title: Rondonia Frontier Showcase Replay
description: Completed mission replay seeded with local Sentinel Hub timelapses, alert evidence, and agent decisions for a compact product walkthrough.
task_text: Replay a completed orbit-to-ground mission across the Rondonia western frontier. Review why the system escalated a subset of cells and how the evidence supports a durable canopy-loss conclusion.
summary: A seeded replay of corridor-style canopy loss across a frontier clearing zone. Four cells are promoted into Inspect with cached timelapses, grounded notes, and agent dialogue so reviewers can follow the decision chain without waiting on a live scan.

alerts.timelapse_analysis: The seeded Sentinel-2 sequence shows intact canopy in early 2023, road-edge opening through mid 2024, and consolidated bare-soil exposure by early 2025 with no seasonal recovery.
alerts.analysis_summary: The central frontier tile transitioned from closed canopy to an established clearing footprint. The persistence and shape of the scar support structural land conversion rather than a transient atmospheric or seasonal effect.
alerts.satellite_note: Orbital triage detected a severe multi-index collapse at the center of the frontier block and requested ground validation.
alerts.ground_note: Ground review confirms persistent canopy removal across the central frontier tile. This cell is the clearest explanation anchor for the replay.
findings: Bare-soil exposure expanded along a road-access edge

alerts.timelapse_analysis: The southern adjacent tile shows the same frontier edge progressing from a narrow opening into a wider disturbed strip over the replay window.
alerts.analysis_summary: This southern neighbor carries the same directional clearing trend as the anchor cell, indicating expansion of the disturbed corridor rather than an isolated artifact.
alerts.satellite_note: Satellite reasoning linked the southern tile to the same corridor-scale disturbance pattern and kept it in the escalation set.
alerts.ground_note: Ground review confirms expansion along the southern edge of the same clearing corridor.
findings: Adjacent clearing expands south of the anchor cell
Moisture loss and soil exposure remain elevated into 2025

alerts.timelapse_analysis: The eastern tile preserves the same directional disturbance but with a smaller final footprint, consistent with edge spread from the core clearing.
alerts.analysis_summary: The east-side evidence is weaker than the anchor cell but still consistent with durable land-cover conversion propagating along the frontier line.
alerts.satellite_note: Satellite triage retained the eastern neighbor as a supporting tile because the vegetation collapse persisted across multiple seeded frames.
alerts.ground_note: Ground review confirms this tile as a supporting extension of the same frontier-clearing story.
findings: Supporting edge-spread signature east of the anchor cell
Recovered less canopy than a seasonal disturbance would suggest

alerts.timelapse_analysis: The northern tile shows a lighter but still persistent canopy-loss edge, useful as a medium-priority example within the same mission footprint.
alerts.analysis_summary: The north-side change is smaller, but it still behaves like a durable frontier extension rather than a short-lived seasonal fluctuation.
alerts.satellite_note: Satellite triage kept the northern edge as a medium-priority flag because the disturbance persisted through the seeded sequence.
alerts.ground_note: Ground review confirms a smaller but still durable canopy-loss extension north of the core clearing.
findings: Medium-priority supporting disturbance north of the anchor cell
Useful comparison tile for confidence calibration
